steps.title: Identify the subject and what the verb needs
steps.content: The subject is the singular noun phrase Food writer J. Kenji López-Alt (the relative clause “who explores the science behind cooking” is a modifier, not the subject). A singular present-tense verb is needed to match this singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong verb forms and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice for singular present-tense agreement:
  • Choice A — are explaining: plural present progressive — does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice B — explains: singular simple present — correctly agrees with the singular subject J. Kenji López-Alt. ✓
  • Choice C — explain: plural simple present — does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — have explained: plural present perfect — does not agree with the singular subject. ✗
That is the correct answer.
